title: elaphoglossum repens
intro: Elaphoglossum repens, also known as the creeping elaphoglossum or creeping lycopodioid fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a small, low-growing plant with delicate fronds and has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Elaphoglossum repens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its potential medicinal and health benefits.
